mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold of 5%, higher borrowing costs for corporations and higher discount rates for future earnings compress equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest FEDFUNDS value is 5.33, above the proposed trigger level.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for negative 20-day returns falls below 55% during periods where FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, which translates into higher aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, supporting equity prices over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent PAYEMS prints have shown continued, albeit moderating, job growth.
failure_condition: A rolling 3-year correlation between the 6-month change in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns turns negative and stays negative for over 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of slowing real output (GDPC1) and weakening consumer spending (RSAFS) signals a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand. This interaction points to an imminent recession, causing a sharp decline in expected corporate earnings and equity prices.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS 3-month average change < 0%
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating 3-month rate of change in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strengthening manufacturing and supply-side activity, which is a positive leading indicator for near-term corporate profitability and drives positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has shown resilience, suggesting the industrial sector is not in a deep contraction.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) begins to rise from a cyclical low while monetary policy is restrictive, markets interpret this as the start of a labor market downturn that will precede a recession, causing a negative repricing of risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has ticked up to 3.9 from recent lows, approaching the 'Sahm Rule' recession indicator threshold.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 experiences a median 20-day forward return greater than zero over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation (CPIAUCSL) forces the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance (high FEDFUNDS), which weighs on equity valuations by increasing the discount rate on future cash flows.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPI inflation remains above the Fed's target while FEDFUNDS is at a cycle high.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is positive during periods when the trigger condition holds for at least two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a strong leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, as it signals falling investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have declined significantly from their post-pandemic peak.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for predicting negative 20-day returns is statistically indistinguishable from 50% (p-value > 0.10) over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.0% indicates a tight labor market and strong household income, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate earnings, creating a tailwind for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The latest UNRATE reading of 3.9 is below the 4.0% threshold.
failure_condition: During periods where UNRATE is below 4.0%, the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is negative over any 2-year rolling window.
